papers.title: Where There’s Smoke: Stochastic Caregiving Shocks and Mothers’ Labor Market Outcomes -- by Joshua S. Graff Zivin, Seunghoon Lee
papers.subtitle: Using plausibly exogenous variation in wildfire smoke exposure, we show that smoke increases school closures and student absenteeism, generating caregiving demand. Formal childcare and public schooling are widely understood to be central to mothers’ labor market attachment.
